senses.definition: separates people again into different groups, especially by race or social class, after they were mixed together
examples: After years of progress, some school districts slowly resegregate along racial lines.
Critics say the new housing policy effectively resegregates low‑income families.

senses.definition: separating people again into different groups, especially by race or social class, after they were mixed together
examples: The report warned that the city was resegregating its schools through new zoning rules.
Activists argued that the plan was quietly resegregating public housing.

senses.definition: the process of separating people again into different groups, especially by race or social class, after they were mixed together
examples: Researchers have documented a pattern of school resegregation in several states.
Local leaders are trying to stop the resegregation of their neighborhoods.

senses.definition: separate instances or types of resegregation
examples: The article compared different resegregations in urban school systems.
Activists are fighting new resegregations in both housing and education.

senses.definition: to put someone back into legal possession of land or property
examples: The court decided to reseise the heir of the disputed estate.
Under the old law, the king could reseise a tenant of his former lands.

senses.definition: a person or authority that puts someone back into legal possession of land or property
examples: The crown acted as reseiser of the confiscated lands.
In the old records, the reseiser is named but the tenant is not.

senses.definition: to put someone back into legal possession of land or property
examples: The court may reseize the owner of land taken without cause.
Under feudal law, the lord could reseize a tenant of his fief.

senses.definition: to take or seize something again
examples: The army moved quickly to reseize the lost territory.
Officials sought to reseize the assets that had been wrongly released.

senses.definition: put back into possession of land, property, or another thing, or taken again
examples: By this judgment, the tenant was reseized of his ancient holding.
The city was reseized by the rebels after a brief ceasefire.

senses.definition: a person or authority that seizes or takes something again
examples: The state acted as reseizer of the disputed funds.
In the records, the reseizer is identified only as a royal officer.

senses.definition: puts someone back into possession of something, or takes something again
examples: The decision reseizes the family of the land they lost.
If the company defaults, the bank reseizes the equipment.

senses.definition: the act of putting someone back into possession of something, or taking something again
examples: The reseizing of the property followed a long legal battle.
The sudden reseizing of the town surprised the enemy.

senses.definition: the act of seizing something again, especially property or assets
examples: The reseizure of the ship was ordered by the court.
Bank records describe the reseizure of several homes after missed payments.

senses.definition: separate acts of seizing something again, especially property or assets
examples: There were several reseizures of cars during the crisis.
These reseizures led to protests from the affected families.

senses.definition: to choose someone or something again
examples: The party decided to reselect her as their candidate.
You can reselect the items you want to buy before checkout.

senses.definition: chosen again, especially for a job or position
examples: She was reselected as team captain for another season.
The mayor was reselected by the party to run in the next election.

senses.definition: choosing someone or something again
examples: The committee is reselecting members for the next term.
You can correct the error by reselecting the correct file.

senses.definition: the act of choosing someone or something again
examples: Her reselection as party leader was expected.
System settings allow the reselection of previously used values.

senses.definition: separate acts of choosing people or things again
examples: A series of reselections will be held across the region.
Software updates may require reselections of default options.

senses.definition: chooses someone or something again
examples: The party often reselects popular candidates.
The program automatically reselects the last used setting.

senses.definition: a person’s self again, especially as it was before some change
examples: After months of recovery, she finally felt like her reself.
Therapy helped him find his reself after the crisis.

senses.definition: to sell something that you bought earlier
examples: They buy old houses, repair them, and resell them at a profit.
Some people resell concert tickets online for higher prices.

senses.definition: a person or company that buys goods and then sells them to other people
examples: Software companies often sell licenses through local resellers.
The ticket reseller charged double the original price.

senses.definition: people or companies that buy goods and then sell them to others
examples: The company works with resellers in over fifty countries.
Many small resellers depend on online marketplaces.

senses.definition: the activity of selling things that you bought earlier
examples: He makes money by reselling second‑hand electronics.
Ticket reselling is restricted by local law.

senses.definition: selling things that you bought earlier
examples: She is reselling her old textbooks online.
The company is reselling licenses to smaller firms.

senses.definition: sells something that was bought earlier
examples: The shop resells returned products at a discount.
He resells rare books to collectors.

senses.definition: able to be resembled or compared in appearance to something
examples: The two models are hardly resemblable at first sight.
These patterns are only faintly resemblable to the original design.

senses.definition: the fact of being similar in appearance or in some other way
examples: There is a strong resemblance between the two sisters.
The new product bears little resemblance to the old model.

senses.definition: things that show similarity in appearance or in some other way
examples: Despite some resemblances, the paintings were done by different artists.
We can see clear resemblances between the two theories.

senses.definition: similar in appearance or character to something else
examples: The two statues are resemblant but not identical.
Their policies are resemblant in several key areas.

senses.definition: to look like or be similar to someone or something
examples: You really resemble your mother.
The clouds resemble mountains on the horizon.

senses.definition: looked like or was similar to someone or something
examples: As a child, he resembled his father.
The sound resembled distant thunder.

senses.definition: a person or thing that looks like or is similar to someone or something else
examples: He is often seen as a close resembler of his famous uncle.
This painting is a poor resembler of the original work.

senses.definition: people or things that look like or are similar to someone or something else
examples: The two actors are remarkable resemblers.
These species are close resemblers, though they live in different regions.

senses.definition: looks like or is similar to someone or something
examples: She closely resembles her grandmother.
This dish resembles the one we had in Italy.

senses.definition: looking like or being similar to someone or something
examples: Children resembling their parents stood in the photo.
She drew shapes resembling stars and moons.

senses.definition: in a way that shows similarity to something else
examples: The two stories are resemblingly structured.
The songs move resemblingly from quiet to loud passages.

senses.definition: to sow seeds again in an area
examples: Farmers decided to reseminate the field after the flood.
We need to reseminate this patch of grass in spring.

senses.definition: to send something again
examples: Could you resend the email? I did not receive it.
If the message fails, the app will automatically resend it.

senses.definition: sending something again
examples: I am resending the document with the correct dates.
The system is resending the verification code.

senses.definition: sends something again
examples: If the email bounces, the server resends it automatically.
She resends the invitation whenever new guests are added.

senses.definition: a mixture of chemical compounds found in rosin and some other natural resins
examples: The sample contained a high level of resene.
Resene is used in studies of natural resin chemistry.

senses.definition: the return of feeling in a part of the body after a loss of sensation
examples: Patients may experience resensation after nerve repair surgery.
The therapist monitored resensation in the injured hand.

senses.definition: to make someone or something sensitive or responsive to something again
examples: The program aims to resensitise people to the impact of violence.
This treatment can resensitise cancer cells to chemotherapy.

senses.definition: made sensitive or responsive to something again
examples: After therapy, she felt resensitised to other people’s feelings.
The drug combination successfully resensitised the tumour cells.

senses.definition: makes someone or something sensitive or responsive to something again
examples: The campaign resensitises the public to environmental problems.
This therapy resensitises the body’s response to insulin.

senses.definition: making someone or something sensitive or responsive to something again
examples: The therapist is resensitising him to normal levels of stress.
Scientists are working on resensitising resistant bacteria to antibiotics.

senses.definition: the process of making someone or something sensitive or responsive to something again
examples: Resensitization of nerve endings may cause temporary discomfort.
The program focuses on the resensitization of people to ethical issues.

senses.definition: to make someone or something sensitive or responsive to something again
examples: The goal of the therapy is to resensitize patients to normal sensations.
This approach may resensitize tumour cells to standard drugs.

senses.definition: made sensitive or responsive to something again
examples: The nerves were gradually resensitized after surgery.
She felt resensitized to the suffering of others.

senses.definition: makes someone or something sensitive or responsive to something again
examples: The treatment resensizes the body to insulin.
Art that shocks can also resensitizes audiences to real‑world violence.
